_comment: Routing model cost-savings study — cheap models for room-by-room LLM routing.

scenes.title: Routing Model Cost Study
scenes.subtitle: Room-by-room cheap routing, backed by mined Kitsoki turns.

scenes.body: Can Haiku-class, synthetic small, and GPT mini models replace Sonnet for residual LLM-tier routing — including hard-negative correction turns?
scenes.lede: Kitsoki already routes most turns deterministically at $0. The cost lever is the residual LLM tier, evaluated room-by-room against real mined inputs, not as a global downgrade.
scenes.narration: Kitsoki routes sixty-two percent of turns at zero cost. Only the thirty-seven percent residual reaches the LLM — that is what we want to make cheaper.

scenes.title: The eight-tier routing stack
scenes.caption: The first five tiers cost nothing. The LLM fires only on the genuinely ambiguous residual — 37.5% of Oregon Trail turns (calibration gate ≤40%).
scenes.narration: Seven tiers cost zero. The LLM only fires for the residual. That is the only spend to optimise.

scenes.title: Mined real session inputs
scenes.caption: The broad mine is the case-study corpus. Oregon Trail and merge_judge provide existing structured eval evidence.
scenes.narration: The report uses one hundred fifteen real Kitsoki sessions, four hundred sixty-one routing candidates, and forty-seven correction hard negatives.

scenes.title: Harness profiles
scenes.caption: ★ = machine default. GPT mini-style comparison is requested but not configured — add a profile before claiming evidence.
scenes.narration: Four profiles are configured. GPT mini is a configuration gap — no profile, no measurement.

scenes.eyebrow: merge_judge call-site — only measured routing evidence
scenes.title: Candidate comparison
scenes.caption: Bar = 95% pass / 8000ms p95 / $0.0020 avg cost. A 0% bar-pass row can still show non-zero comparator effectiveness — these are different metrics.
scenes.narration: Synthetic small is 4.25 times cheaper than Sonnet and 41 percent faster on p95 latency. It is the committed selection. Haiku and GPT mini lack evidence.

scenes.body: slot-template routing handles all Oregon Trail correction turns at zero cost — but the one documented LLM-tier hard negative failed on both small and large synthetic models.
scenes.lede: Correction fixtures in state:general_store.reviewing ('actually give me 12 oxen', 'I want 7 oxen not 6') route via synonym template at 100% — no model involved. The merge_judge failure on missing-merge-sha-blocks-closeout shows that when a correction turn does reach the LLM tier, syn:small:text can misclassify: returned accept when refine was expected. The committed fallback profile (claude) catches this case.
scenes.narration: Most correction turns route at zero cost via templates. The one LLM-tier hard negative is covered by the stronger fallback.

scenes.title: Declared bar vs. measured medians
scenes.caption: A 'divergence' row passed the report's own check but violates the declared dataset bar. No candidate fully satisfies the declared merge_judge bar at its declared thresholds.
scenes.narration: No single candidate satisfies every threshold. The committed decision is cheapest-passing with a fallback arc for the edge case.

scenes.title: Sweep blocked — per-example confidence not recorded

cells: Loaded eval reports do not include per-example actual confidence values
Each example result needs: actual.intent, actual.confidence, expected intent
Cannot recommend a lower confidence bar with measured false-accept rates
Instrument eval runners to record per-example results; rerun this report

scenes.caption: Until per-example confidence values are recorded, threshold sweep cannot be run.
scenes.narration: The confidence-threshold sweep is blocked. We cannot yet recommend a lower confidence bar with statistical backing.

scenes.title: Projected residual routing cost by model
scenes.caption: Measured values from merge_judge. Haiku and GPT-4o-mini are published-rate estimates, not measured call-site results.
scenes.narration: At one thousand turns per day, synthetic small saves about one dollar per day versus Sonnet — four-point-two-five times cheaper. Haiku and GPT mini estimates suggest further headroom.

scenes.title: Readiness status by story

cells: gate, existing_scan, references_research, name_slug, draft, verify-agent

scenes.caption: 10 of 11 mining-profiled stories have no loaded coverage job. All 13 unmeasured call sites block a validated room-by-room routing switch.
scenes.narration: Only one call site has any evidence. Thirteen more are planned but unmeasured.

scenes.body: Recommendation: promote cheap routers only per room after they pass mined hard negatives. Keep Sonnet-class fallback and route receipts.
scenes.lede: Synthetic small is the cheapest measured candidate. Haiku is the conservative first cheap sweep. GPT mini remains a configuration gap. The architecture decision is room-by-room selection, not global downgrading.
scenes.narration: The recommendation is room-by-room promotion with fallback, not a global downgrade.

scenes.title: Selection proposal

cells: Only candidate with 100% acceptance-bar pass rate on its own report pass flag
Committed cheapest_passing decision; claude fallback for hard-negative edge cases

scenes.caption: apply_mode=report — no override written. Configuration unchanged. Haiku and GPT mini are gaps; add to eval matrices and run with operator approval.
scenes.narration: The selection is synthetic small with a Sonnet fallback arc. No config override was applied — this is a report run.

scenes.body: 1. Feed .artifacts/routing-model-corpus into a routing eval. 2. Add GPT mini profile. 3. Run Haiku and synthetic small on the same hard negatives. 4. Record confidence and alternatives. 5. Promote passing rooms with fallback.
scenes.lede: The case study produced the corpus and decision frame. The next implementation step is an eval runner that turns these mined inputs into per-room promotion evidence.
scenes.narration: Five concrete next steps: turn the mined corpus into a routing eval and promote only the rooms that pass.
